heading: Coverage requirements for children’s health insurance
label: 1397cc Coverage requirements for children’s health insurance
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (c)(10), was in the original “the date of the enactment of this subparagraph”, and was translated as if it had read “the date of the enactment of this paragraph” to reflect the probable intent of Congress.March 18, 2020The Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, referred to in subsec. children.heading: Required scope of health insurance coverage
children.label: a Required scope of health insurance coverage

children.heading: Benchmark coverage
children.label: 1 Benchmark coverage
children.text: Health benefits coverage that is at least equivalent to the benefits coverage in a benchmark benefit package described in subsection (b).

children.heading: Inclusion of basic services
children.label: A Inclusion of basic services
children.text: The coverage includes benefits for items and services within each of the categories of basic services described in subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: Aggregate actuarial value equivalent to benchmark package
children.label: B Aggregate actuarial value equivalent to benchmark package
children.text: The coverage has an aggregate actuarial value that is at least actuarially equivalent to one of the benchmark benefit packages.

children.heading: Substantial actuarial value for additional services included in benchmark package
children.label: C Substantial actuarial value for additional services included in benchmark package
children.text: With respect to each of the categories of additional services described in subsection (c)(2) for which coverage is provided under the benchmark benefit package used under subparagraph (B), the coverage has an actuarial value that is equal to at least 75 percent of the actuarial value of the coverage of that category of services in such package.

children.heading: Existing comprehensive State-based coverage
children.label: 3 Existing comprehensive State-based coverage
children.text: Health benefits coverage under an existing comprehensive State-based program, described in subsection (d)(1).

children.heading: Secretary-approved coverage
children.label: 4 Secretary-approved coverage
children.text: Any other health benefits coverage that the Secretary determines, upon application by a State, provides appropriate coverage for the population of targeted low-income children proposed to be provided such coverage.

children.heading: FEHBP-equivalent children’s health insurance coverage
children.label: 1 FEHBP-equivalent children’s health insurance coverage
children.text: The standard Blue Cross/Blue Shield preferred provider option service benefit plan, described in and offered under .section 8903(1) of title 5

children.heading: State employee coverage
children.label: 2 State employee coverage
children.text: A health benefits coverage plan that is offered and generally available to State employees in the State involved.

children.label: A
children.text: is offered by a health maintenance organization (as defined in section 2791(b)(3) of the Public Health Service Act []), and42 U.S.C. 300gg–91(b)(3)

children.label: B
children.text: has the largest insured commercial, non-medicaid enrollment of covered lives of such coverage plans offered by such a health maintenance organization in the State involved.

children.heading: Categories of services; determination of actuarial value of coverage
children.label: c Categories of services; determination of actuarial value of coverage
A
children.text: Inpatient and outpatient hospital services.

children.label: B
children.text: Physicians’ surgical and medical services.

children.label: C
children.text: Laboratory and x-ray services.

children.label: D
children.text: Well-baby and well-child care, including age-appropriate immunizations.

children.label: E
children.text: Mental health and substance use disorder services (as defined in paragraph (5)).

children.label: A
children.text: Coverage of prescription drugs.

children.label: B
children.text: Vision services.

children.label: C
children.text: Hearing services.

children.heading: Treatment of other categories
children.label: 3 Treatment of other categories
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed as preventing a State child health plan from providing coverage of benefits that are not within a category of services described in paragraph (1) or (2).

children.label: A
children.text: by an individual who is a member of the American Academy of Actuaries;

children.label: B
children.text: using generally accepted actuarial principles and methodologies;

children.label: C
children.text: using a standardized set of utilization and price factors;

children.label: D
children.text: using a standardized population that is representative of privately insured children of the age of children who are expected to be covered under the State child health plan;

children.label: E
children.text: applying the same principles and factors in comparing the value of different coverage (or categories of services);

children.label: F
children.text: without taking into account any differences in coverage based on the method of delivery or means of cost control or utilization used; and

children.label: G
children.text: taking into account the ability of a State to reduce benefits by taking into account the increase in actuarial value of benefits coverage offered under the State child health plan that results from the limitations on cost sharing under such coverage.

children.heading: Mental health and substance use disorder services
children.label: 5 Mental health and substance use disorder services
A
children.text: include coverage of mental health services (including behavioral health treatment) necessary to prevent, diagnose, and treat a broad range of mental health symptoms and disorders, including substance use disorders; and

children.label: B
children.text: be delivered in a culturally and linguistically appropriate manner.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The child health assistance provided to a targeted low-income child shall include coverage of dental services necessary to prevent disease and promote oral health, restore oral structures to health and function, and treat emergency conditions.

children.heading: Permitting use of dental benchmark plans by certain States
children.label: B Permitting use of dental benchmark plans by certain States
children.text: A State may elect to meet the requirement of subparagraph (A) through dental coverage that is equivalent to a benchmark dental benefit package described in subparagraph (C).

children.heading: FEHBP children’s dental coverage
children.label: i FEHBP children’s dental coverage
children.text: A dental benefits plan under chapter 89A of title 5 that has been selected most frequently by employees seeking dependent coverage, among such plans that provide such dependent coverage, in either of the previous 2 plan years.

children.heading: State employee dependent dental coverage
children.label: ii State employee dependent dental coverage
children.text: A dental benefits plan that is offered and generally available to State employees in the State involved and that has been selected most frequently by employees seeking dependent coverage, among such plans that provide such dependent coverage, in either of the previous 2 plan years.

children.heading: Coverage offered through commercial dental plan
children.label: iii Coverage offered through commercial dental plan
children.text: A dental benefits plan that has the largest insured commercial, non-medicaid enrollment of dependent covered lives of such plans that is offered in the State involved.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A State child health plan shall ensure that the financial requirements and treatment limitations applicable to mental health and substance use disorder services (as described in paragraph (5)) provided under such plan comply with the requirements of section 2726(a) of the Public Health Service Act [] in the same manner as such requirements or limitations apply to a group health plan under such section. In applying the previous sentence with respect to requirements under paragraph (8) of section 2726(a) of the Public Health Service Act [], a State child health plan described in such sentence shall be treated as in compliance with such requirements if the State child health plan is in compliance with section 457.496 of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation.42 U.S.C. 300gg–26(a)42 U.S.C. 300gg–26(a)

children.heading: Deemed compliance
children.label: B Deemed compliance
children.text: To the extent that a State child health plan includes coverage with respect to an individual described in and covered under the State plan under of the services described in (relating to early and periodic screening, diagnostic, and treatment services defined in ) and provided in accordance with , such plan shall be deemed to satisfy the requirements of subparagraph (A).section 1396d(a)(4)(B) of this titlesection 1396a(a)(10)(A) of this titlesection 1396d(a)(4)(B) of this titlesection 1396d(r) of this titlesection 1396a(a)(43) of this title

children.heading: Construction on prohibited coverage
children.label: 8 Construction on prohibited coverage
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as requiring any health benefits coverage offered under the plan to provide coverage for items or services for which payment is prohibited under this subchapter, notwithstanding that any benchmark benefit package includes coverage for such an item or service.

children.heading: Availability of coverage for items and services furnished through school-based health centers
children.label: 9 Availability of coverage for items and services furnished through school-based health centers
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter shall be construed as limiting a State’s ability to provide child health assistance for covered items and services that are furnished through school-based health centers (as defined in ).section 1397jj(c)(9) of this title

children.heading: Certain in vitro diagnostic products for COVID–19 testing
children.label: 10 Certain in vitro diagnostic products for COVID–19 testing
children.text: The child health assistance provided to a targeted low-income child shall include coverage of any in vitro diagnostic product described in that is administered during any portion of the emergency period described in such section beginning on or after (and the administration of such product).section 1396d(a)(3)(B) of this title1March 18, 2020See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Required coverage of COVID–19 vaccines and treatment
children.label: 11 Required coverage of COVID–19 vaccines and treatment
A
children.text: a COVID–19 vaccine (and the administration of the vaccine); and

children.label: B
children.text: testing and treatments for COVID–19, including specialized equipment and therapies (including preventive therapies), and, in the case of an individual who is diagnosed with or presumed to have COVID–19, during the period during which such individual has (or is presumed to have) COVID–19, the treatment of a condition that may seriously complicate the treatment of COVID–19, if otherwise covered under the State child health plan (or waiver of such plan).

children.heading: Required coverage of approved, recommended adult vaccines and their administration.
children.label: 12 Required coverage of approved, recommended adult vaccines and their administration.
children.text: Regardless of the type of coverage elected by a State under subsection (a), if the State child health plan or a waiver of such plan provides child health assistance or pregnancy-related assistance (as defined in section 1397 of this title) to an individual who is 19 years of age or older, such assistance shall include coverage of vaccines described in and their administration.llsection 1396d(a)(13)(B) of this title

children.heading: Description of existing comprehensive State-based coverage
children.label: d Description of existing comprehensive State-based coverage
A
children.text: includes coverage of a range of benefits;

children.label: B
children.text: is administered or overseen by the State and receives funds from the State;

children.label: C
children.text: is offered in New York, Florida, or Pennsylvania; and

children.label: D
children.text: was offered as of .August 5, 1997

children.label: A
children.text: the actuarial value of the coverage under the program as of , orAugust 5, 1997

children.label: B
children.text: the actuarial value described in subsection (a)(2)(B),

children.heading: Description
children.label: A Description
children.text: A State child health plan shall include a description, consistent with this subsection, of the amount (if any) of premiums, deductibles, coinsurance, and other cost sharing imposed. Any such charges shall be imposed pursuant to a public schedule.

children.heading: Protection for lower income children
children.label: B Protection for lower income children
children.text: The State child health plan may only vary premiums, deductibles, coinsurance, and other cost sharing based on the family income of targeted low-income children in a manner that does not favor children from families with higher income over children from families with lower income.

children.heading: No cost sharing on benefits for preventive services, COVID–19 testing, a COVID–19 vaccine, COVID–19 treatment, or pregnancy-related assistance
children.label: 2 No cost sharing on benefits for preventive services, COVID–19 testing, a COVID–19 vaccine, COVID–19 treatment, or pregnancy-related assistance
children.text: The State child health plan may not impose deductibles, coinsurance, or other cost sharing with respect to benefits for services within the categories of services described in subsection (c)(1)(D), in vitro diagnostic products described in subsection (c)(10) (and administration of such products), vaccines described in subsection (c)(12) (and the administration of such vaccines), services described in section 1396(a)(2)(G) of this title, vaccines described in section 1396(a)(2)(H) of this title administered during the period described in such section (and the administration of such vaccines), testing or treatments described in section 1396(a)(2)(I) of this title furnished during the period described in such section, or for pregnancy-related assistance.ooo

children.heading: Children in families with income below 150 percent of poverty line
children.label: A Children in families with income below 150 percent of poverty line
i
children.text: an enrollment fee, premium, or similar charge that exceeds the maximum monthly charge permitted consistent with standards established to carry out section 1396(b)(1) of this title (with respect to individuals described in such section); ando

children.label: ii
children.text: a deductible, cost sharing, or similar charge that exceeds an amount that is nominal (as determined consistent with regulations referred to in section 1396(a)(3) of this title, with such appropriate adjustment for inflation or other reasons as the Secretary determines to be reasonable).o

children.heading: Other children
children.label: B Other children
children.text: For children not described in subparagraph (A), subject to paragraphs (1)(B) and (2), any premiums, deductibles, cost sharing or similar charges imposed under the State child health plan may be imposed on a sliding scale related to income, except that the total annual aggregate cost-sharing with respect to all targeted low-income children in a family under this subchapter may not exceed 5 percent of such family’s income for the year involved.

children.label: i
children.text: shall afford individuals enrolled under the plan a grace period of at least 30 days from the beginning of a new coverage period to make premium payments before the individual’s coverage under the plan may be terminated; and

children.label: I
children.text: that failure to make a premium payment within the grace period will result in termination of coverage under the State child health plan; and

children.label: II
children.text: of the individual’s right to challenge the proposed termination pursuant to the applicable Federal regulations.

children.heading: Relation to medicaid requirements
children.label: 4 Relation to medicaid requirements
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed as affecting the rules relating to the use of enrollment fees, premiums, deductions, cost sharing, and similar charges in the case of targeted low-income children who are provided child health assistance in the form of coverage under a medicaid program under .section 1397aa(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Restriction on application of preexisting condition exclusions
children.label: 1 Restriction on application of preexisting condition exclusions

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the State child health plan shall not permit the imposition of any preexisting condition exclusion for covered benefits under the plan.

children.heading: Group health plans and group health insurance coverage
children.label: B Group health plans and group health insurance coverage
children.text: If the State child health plan provides for benefits through payment for, or a contract with, a group health plan or group health insurance coverage, the plan may permit the imposition of a preexisting condition exclusion but only insofar as it is permitted under the applicable provisions of part 7 of subtitle B of title I of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 [ et seq.] and title XXVII of the Public Health Service Act [ et seq.].29 U.S.C. 118142 U.S.C. 300gg

children.heading: Compliance with other requirements
children.label: 2 Compliance with other requirements
children.text: Coverage offered under this section shall comply with the requirements of subpart 2 of part A of title XXVII of the Public Health Service Act insofar as such requirements apply with respect to a health insurance issuer that offers group health insurance coverage.1

children.heading: Compliance with managed care requirements
children.label: 3 Compliance with managed care requirements
children.text: The State child health plan shall provide for the application of subsections (a)(4), (a)(5), (b), (c), (d), (e), and (j) of (relating to requirements for managed care) to coverage, State agencies, enrollment brokers, managed care entities, and managed care organizations under this subchapter in the same manner as such subsections apply to coverage and such entities and organizations under subchapter XIX.section 1396u–2 of this title
